nodes.x: But Job answered and said,
But Job answered and said,
Then Job answered and said,
And Job answered and said,
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?
He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, and high is thy right hand.
¶ And he saw that there was no man, and wondered that there was no intercessor: therefore his arm brought salvation unto him; and his righteousness, it sustained him.
The Lord is their strength, and he is the saving strength of his anointed.
For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.
How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?
Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
The way of a fool is right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel is wise.

Wherefore, brethren, seek not to counsel the Lord, but to take counsel from his hand. For behold, ye yourselves know that he counseleth in wisdom, and in justice, and in great mercy, over all his works.
Behold, thou desirest truth in the inward parts: and in the hidden part thou shalt make me to know wisdom.
To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
And the Lord said unto him: Believest thou the words which I shall speak?

And I will come down and talk with thee there: and I will take of the spirit which is upon thee, and will put it upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with thee, that thou bear it not thyself alone.
Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.
That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?
Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.

minutes after they are in the bowels of the shark, yet those persons will come forth in the resurrection, and receive all the glory of which they are worthy, and be clothed upon with all the beauty of resurrected Saints, as much so as if they had been laid away in a gold or silver coffin, and in a place expressly for burying the dead. If you think opposite to this your thoughts are in vain. “And…
And it came to pass that they did cast their dead into the waters of Sidon, and they have gone forth and are buried in the depths of the sea.
And the third part of the creatures which were in the sea, and had life, died; and the third part of the ships were destroyed.

enveloped in a mass of waters; it was called forth from the womb of liquid elements. Here was the first birth of our creation, — the waters rolled back, and the dry land appeared, and was soon clothed upon with vegetable and animal existence. This was similar to all other births; being first encompassed in a flood of mighty waters, it burst forth from them, and was soon clothed with all the…
Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.
Thy nakedness shall be uncovered, yea, thy shame shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and I will not meet thee as a man.
For that is his covering only, it is his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I am gracious.
I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.

37 (return) [ Mr. Reland notes here very truly, that the word naked does
not always signify entirely naked, but sometimes without men's usual
armor, without heir usual robes or upper garments; as when Virgil bids
the husbandman plough naked, and sow naked; when Josephus says [Antiq.
B. IV. ch. 3. sect. 2: that God had given the Jews the security of armor
when they were naked; and when he here…
He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;

For, lo, he that formeth the mountains, and createth the wind, and declareth unto man what is his thought, that maketh the morning darkness, and treadeth upon the high places of the earth, The Lord, The God of hosts, is his name.

And all the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing: and he doeth according to his will in the army of heaven, and among the inhabitants of the earth: and none can stay his hand, or say unto him, What doest thou?
Behold, the Lord maketh the earth empty, and maketh it waste, and turneth it upside down, and scattereth abroad the inhabitants thereof.
And he dwelleth in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heaps.
He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.
With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.
By his knowledge the depths are broken up, and the clouds drop down the dew.
Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.
He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens; and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth: he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.
He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.
Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.
And it came to pass in those days, that he went out into a mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
Then brought he me out of the way of the gate northward, and led me about the way without unto the utter gate by the way that looketh eastward; and, behold, there ran out waters on the right side.
And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
When he prepared the heavens, I was there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth:
The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.

And in fine, wo unto all those who tremble, and are angry because of the truth of God! For behold, he that is built upon the rock receiveth it with gladness; and he that is built upon a sandy foundation trembleth lest he shall fall.
And then shall the Lord set his foot upon this mount, and it shall cleave in twain, and the earth shall tremble, and reel to and fro, and the heavens also shall shake.

reason why we do not see Him—He is too pure a being to show himself to the eyes of mortals; He has overcome, and goes no more out, but He is the temple of my God, and is a pillar there. What is a pillar? It is that power which supports the superstructure which bears up the edifice; and if that should be removed from its place, the edifice is in danger of falling. Hence, our heavenly Father…

And behold the voice came again, saying: Repent ye, repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand; and seek no more to destroy my servants. And it came to pass that the earth shook again, and the walls trembled.
And then shall he tremble, and shall look pale, even as if death had come upon him.
He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.
He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.
The sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.
Bless, Lord, his substance, and accept the work of his hands: smite through the loins of them that rise against him, and of them that hate him, that they rise not again.
By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
And he laid hold on the dragon, that old serpent, which is the Devil, and Satan, and bound him a thousand years,

unto me—Father, thy will be done, and the glory be thine forever. Wherefore, because that Satan rebelled against me, and sought to destroy the agency of man, which I, the Lord God, had given him, and also, that I should give unto him mine own power, by the power of mine Only Begotten, I caused that he should be cast down; And he became Satan, yea, even the devil, the father of all lies, to…
And he said, Cast it on the ground. And he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses fled from before it.
Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?
God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.
